Rural Development funds will be used to help Northeastern Technical College with campuses in Cheraw, SC and Dillon, SC to establish a distance learning project that will provide virtual connections to classrooms located in Dillon, Lake View and Latta, SC. The hubs and end-user sites are located in Chesterfield and Dillon Counties. The project will allow the project schools to connect to other counties, located near and/or far, and connect to other schools, colleges, and organizations in order to bring needed educational content to teachers and students. The project will allow for expanded educational opportunities to provide students with core courses, electives, dual credit courses, and advanced placement and will also allow for community meetings, continuing education, and training for first responders.

Rural Development funds will be used to help create a distance learning network in Marlboro and Chesterfield Counties, South Carolina. Northeastern Technical College campuses at Bennettsville and Pageland, SC will be the hub/end-user sites, and high schools at Pageland, Chesterfield, McBee and Cheraw will be end-user sites. Expanded course offerings will include core course, dual credit courses and advanced placement courses. The Learning Network will also provide a community resource for workforce development, job training, community meetings and continuing education for first responders. Advanced manufacturing mechatronics type courses will also be offered, which will enhance local manufacturing job opportunities.

Rural Development funds will be used to help Avera Health located in Sioux Falls, SD implement a telehealth project to enhance labor and delivery and post-partum services for mothers and their babies. The end-user sites are eleven rural hospitals in South Dakota, Iowa and Minnesota. The new equipment will be able to monitor a babys heart rate, which is a critical indicator of fetal health during the birthing process. When necessary, the babys heart rate information can be transported to Avera Health, 24 hours a day, where specialists can assist at a critical time for mother and baby. Averas Best Beginnings Rural Obstetrics Connections Project will reduce brain damages, permanent injuries and even deaths in difficult pregnancies when something goes wrong and additional help is immediately needed. This project will greatly improve labor and delivery services in rural hospitals by making highly specialized and dedicated specialists available whenever they are needed.

Rural Development funds will help Avera Health implement an advanced telehealth project to provide access to telehealth pharmacy services. Avera will connect to end-user sites in Hutchinson and Spink Counties in South Dakota, Osceola, Iowa and Lincoln, Minnesota with their hub in Minnehaha, South Dakota. The project will provide telehealth programs with 24-hour pharmacist support for review of medication orders. This is a crucial step to prevent medication errors related to allergies, drug interactions and incorrect dosages.

Rural Development funds will help Chattanooga State Community College located in Chattanooga, TN develop a distance learning program that will connect to schools in Kimball and Dayton, TN. The distance learning project involving more than 55 classes will include academic advising, financial aid sessions, academic success coaching, as well as intervention and teacher meetings. Additionally, the community via connection with Chattanooga States main campus will be able to see and hear visiting speakers, authors, and scholars presentations

Borrower Name: United South and Eastern Tribes, Inc.
Project Announced Description: Rural Development funds will help the United South and Eastern Tribes, Inc. (USET) purchase telemedicine equipment to provide enhanced telemedicine service. The project will benefit one hub and 10 end-user sites in 10 communities throughout Tennessee, Maine, and Florida. It will provide computer servers, video conferencing equipment, laptops and tablets, electronic stethoscopes and HORUS scopes, software and networking equipment. USET is planning to expand its offering to Tribal Nation health departments to include direct service provision and support through professional development and telemedicine, initially in psychiatry and behavioral health. The goal is to establish a technology platform that facilitates quality health care for tribal citizens and builds the capacity of tribally operated health clinics to ensure high performance through professional development and improved efficiency.
Detailed Project Description Not Available

Rural Development funds will be used to help the Texas Rural Education Association in Camp County purchase video conferencing equipment to provide interactive distance learning service. The project will benefit two hub and 10 end-user sites located in 10 communities by providing computer servers, video conferencing equipment, software and networking components to allow schools to provide a rich array of synchronous distance learning experiences. These opportunities will include dual enrollment classes, virtual field trips, classroom collaborations, career explorations, and an expanded menu of course offerings at all levels. Teachers will be able to participate in professional learning communities and access development opportunities without travel.

Rural Development Funds will be used to help the Texas Rural Education Association located in Travis County purchase video conferencing equipment to provide interactive distance learning services. The project will benefit two hub and 12 end-user sites in nine communities by providing computer servers, network equipment, video conferencing equipment, laptop computers and software to allow the schools to provide dual enrollment courses, virtual field trips, classroom collaborations, career explorations and an expanded menu of courses at all levels. Teachers will be able to access professional development courses and learning communities without the hardship of long travel.
Detailed Project Description Not Available

Rural Development funds will help the University of Washington in King County, WA purchase telemedicine equipment to provide enhanced telemedicine services for its The RIDE (Regional Initiatives in Dental Education) tele-dentistry program. The project will benefit one hub and 11 end-user sites in 12 communities, throughout Washington and Montana, by providing tele dentistry mobile carts, intraoral cameras, video conferencing equipment and software. The RIDE program trains students to be placed in rural dental practices after they graduate. It was developed to address oral health workforce needs in rural and underserved communities. RIDE students are sent on rural service learning rotations for five to six months during their four years of dental school. While all dental students go on five-week rotations, only RIDE students are required to do so in rural locations for a prolonged period of time. The equipment purchased will allow the students to continue their education while serving in the rural communities.
